subject: [Policy] Use common mariadb galera chart
commit_message:
[Policy] Use common mariadb galera chart

Instead of using "own" galera chart, reuse chart placed in common part
of OOM installation.
Will ease move to common galera cluster (which is not done by this
commit).

Change-Id: I2d7c1e5cdc9289cfb55e288b1697373239ef96e3
Issue-ID: POLICY-1467
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

Patch Set 9:

Hello,
After some tries and errors, this patchset is working (gating should show it soon).
What I did on top of removing mariadb chart and using common galera chart:

- taken all passwords found from variables
- use secret for passwords set as environment variable
- pdp and brmsgw were waiting for "pap" which doesn't exists (anymore?). I change the nameOverride of pap to "policy" as main subchart was renamed pap

Please take a look and tell me if that suits you!

Patch Set 10: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Thanks Sylvain, nice work.  It looks and runs nice.  

One observation is when I run the "helm del --purge dev-policy", it left the mariadb-galera "pv" terminating and "pvc" pointing to it.   I manually deleted the pvc afterwards to fully clean it up, before re-installing dev-policy again. FYI.  

Some feedback on your previous note with regards of the renaming of "pap" to "policy", I guess the "pap" before referred to the "legacy" architecture, so we run 2 paps, the old one and the new one.  Eventually the legacy one would be deprecated, the hierarchical composition of the helm chart is anecdotal/historical.   Eventually we need to reorganize when those legacy components are deprecated.   I don't think there are side effects with the renaming change, so no issue.
Change has been successfully merged by Borislav Glozman
